is_p_falciparum.evidence: Discusses Plasmodium falciparum antifolate resistance throughout.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Mentions dhfr codons 51, 59, 108, 164 and dhps 437, 540 in commentary text.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: This is a leading article/commentary; it narratively describes another study's findings (14 of ~500 cases carried I164L) but provides no per-locus frequency tables of its own.

sub_country_location.evidence: Only refers to 'an area of intense perennial transmission in Kenya' — no sub-national breakdown tied to frequencies; data belong to the Hamel et al. primary study.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No collection dates given for any data in this commentary.

african_field_samples.evidence: Discusses Kenyan clinical isolates, but as reported by other studies.

is_primary_study.evidence: This is a 'LEADING ARTICLE' commentary reviewing Hamel et al. and other studies, not a primary empirical study.

duplicate_risk.evidence: It is a commentary summarizing the primary study by Hamel et al. (this issue) and McCollum et al. (2006); any data it mentions belong to those published papers.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary data would make this commentary a primary data source; the actual data are in Hamel et al. and McCollum et al.

assessment.summary: This is a leading article (commentary) by J.E. Hyde discussing antifolate resistance and the dhfr I164L quadruple mutant in Africa, summarizing findings from Hamel et al. and other primary studies. It is not a primary empirical study and reports no original extractable per-locus frequencies with sub-country or temporal resolution.
exclusion_reasons: Not a primary study — it is a commentary/leading article
No original extractable per-locus frequency data
No sub-country spatial resolution tied to frequencies
No collection time information for any data
Data described belong to other published studies (duplicate/derivative)
